hook: Kai stands in the hallway with a drag mark at his feet and a dark staircase above him, and he must choose: go up, or go back.
goal: Find the correct floorboard in the back room.

start_state.location: The hallway of the Old House (loc_001)
items: bag of cleaning supplies (including a screwdriver)
house key given by Mr. Holt
knowledge: A frightened family hid a metal box under a floorboard in the back room
The tape shows the hiding, but the correct board was not identified in Episode 1
A long straight drag mark appeared in the hallway dust between his arrival and departure in Episode 1
suspicions: Someone else was in the house during or after his last visit
The box may still be under the floor

continuity_rules: Must open at the moment Kai turns away from the staircase — do not re-stage the drag mark discovery as a new event
Camera is already in his bag from Episode 1 — do not describe finding it again
Must not reveal the intruder's identity or connect them to any named character yet
Must not open the box — that is Episode 3's beat
Holt's phone manner should be curt and dismissive, not obviously sinister — the HOLT connection is reserved for Episode 3
key_events: Kai stares at the dark staircase but does not climb it — the box is his priority, and the drag mark has already unnerved him. He turns away and walks back to the back room.

In the back room, Kai replays the tape on the camera screen very slowly. This time he notices a detail he missed before: the board the family knelt beside, in the corner nearest the window, has a thin crack running across it. He looks at the real floor and finds the matching board — the crack is still there. He is certain this is the right one.

Before Kai can try to lift the board, he sees a fresh bootprint in the dust near the cupboard. It is wide and flat. His own boot makes a narrower print beside it. Someone else entered this room after his last visit. Kai does not leave. His curiosity is stronger than his fear.

Kai phones Mr. Holt and asks if anyone else has a key to the house. Holt says no — only Kai has one. Holt asks why Kai is asking. Kai says he noticed some marks in the dust. Holt says old houses are full of dust and tells Kai to finish the job. He ends the call before Kai can say anything more.

Kai presses the screwdriver into the edge of the cracked board and pushes hard. The board does not move. It is nailed in tight on every side. He needs a crowbar — a tool he does not have with him. He puts the screwdriver back, picks up his bag, and leaves the house.
major_reveal: Kai identifies the correct floorboard — the cracked board in the corner of the back room nearest the window — by matching a detail on the tape to the real floor.
threads_advanced: thread_001 — Kai now knows the exact board beneath which the box is hidden
thread_002 — the fresh bootprint confirms the unknown person has returned to the back room since Kai's last visit, making the threat active rather than past
new_thread_created: thread_003 — Does the unknown person already know which floorboard hides the metal box?
tone_guardrails: The intruder must remain entirely off-screen — only their physical trace is visible
Holt's curtness on the phone should read as an employer being impatient, not a villain being evasive
Do not stack suspicion on Holt — keep the dramatic irony light at this stage
forbidden_moves: Do not show Kai climbing or exploring the staircase in this episode
Do not reveal the identity of the person who made the bootprint or the drag mark
Do not open or move the floorboard — Kai cannot lift it
Do not re-present the drag mark as a fresh discovery — Kai already knows it was there
Do not make Holt's reaction on the phone feel threatening — save that tension for Episode 3 and beyond
vocabulary_targets: crack — a thin break or line running across a hard surface
footprint — the mark a shoe or boot leaves in dust or soft ground
corner — the place inside a room where two walls meet
tight — fixed very firmly so it cannot easily move
crowbar — a heavy metal bar used to force open or lift something
ending_hook: Kai knows exactly where the box is — but someone else keeps coming back to that same room, and he cannot be sure they do not already know the same thing.
